title: longint — arbitrary precision
manager: An arbitrary-precision integer for when 53 bits isn't enough and you refuse to lose a digit — big factorials, cryptographic-scale counters, exact combinatorics. It trades the provable finiteness of the sized ints for unbounded range, so by design it sits outside the model-checkable band; you reach for it precisely when correctness of the number matters more than decidability of the machine.
cs: arbitrary-precision (BigInt-backed); `L` literal suffix; not in the finite band; promotion into and out of it is explicit only.
